cards.definition: A junction that seals the space between epithelial cells to prevent leakage.
A cell junction that connects actin filaments between neighboring cells.
A junction that connects intermediate filaments between cells for structural support.
A channel that allows small molecules and ions to pass between cells.
A structure that anchors cells to the extracellular matrix.
A calcium-dependent protein that mediates cell–cell adhesion.
A cadherin important in epithelial cells and early embryonic development.
Binding between identical molecules, such as cadherin to cadherin.
Binding between different types of molecules.
The process by which cells sort themselves based on adhesion strength.
Cytoskeletal structures involved in cell shape and adhesion.
A ring of adherens junctions that can contract to change cell shape.
The inward folding of a sheet of cells during development.
The reorganization of tissues during development or repair.
The process of cell growth, DNA replication, and division.
The stage of the cell cycle where the cell grows and replicates DNA.
A phase of cell growth and environmental monitoring.
The phase in which DNA replication occurs.
The phase where the cell prepares for mitosis.
The phase that includes mitosis and cytokinesis.
The division of the nucleus.
The division of the cytoplasm.
Signals that stimulate cell division.
Signals that increase cell mass and promote protein synthesis.
Signals that prevent programmed cell death.
Cell signaling over short distances between nearby cells.
Errors in DNA caused by replication issues or external factors.
A tumor suppressor protein that halts the cell cycle when DNA is damaged.
A structure of DNA and protein that carries genetic information.
The protective end of a chromosome that shortens with each division.
An enzyme that maintains and extends telomeres.
The process by which cells stop dividing after many cycles.
Programmed cell death.
A halt in the cell cycle often triggered by DNA damage.
A fluorescent dye used to stain DNA for visualization.
